description: Single source of truth for the python-test job pytest target set, ignore/deselect contract, and xdist parallelization knobs. Consumed by both the pytest execution step in .github/workflows/ci.yml and the ci_test_selection/v1 artifact generator (generate_ci_test_selection_artifact.py) via the scripts/ci/python_test_plan.py loader. Edit this file (not the workflow or the generator) to change the python-test target set.

notes.parallel_exclude_rationale: test_session_manifest_debounce.py was previously quarantined here because it drove a real debounce front-gate with an 80ms window; under xdist CPU saturation the window expired between burst invocations and the coalescing assertion flaked (Issue #1064). It has been rewritten to be wall-clock-independent (Issue #1141): the worker is suppressed via a live lock owned by os.getpid(), events are queued synchronously, and flush is triggered via --flush (blocking call). No time.sleep() or wall-clock assertions remain. The test is now safe to run in the parallel lane. test_skill_runtime_exec_session_manifest.py::test_external_session_manifest_peer_write_does_not_stop_preflight (Issue 1546 AC3) was added to parallel_exclude because its before/after repo-tree snapshot diff can be polluted, under xdist, by tests/test_summarize_agent_transcript.py writing real (non-tmp_path) files under tmp/agent-session-hotspots/ concurrently in another worker. Running the file at -n 0 in the serial lane removes the race without changing the skill_runtime_exec.py module (Out of Scope for Issue 1546).
notes.schemas_tests_inclusion: schemas/tests/ is included exactly once here; it was previously missing from the generator pytest_args, causing the executed target set and the ci_test_selection artifact to drift (Issue #1064).
notes.dedicated_lanes_excluded: tests/codex/test_local_main_branch_guard.py runs in its own codex execpolicy step (needs the pinned codex CLI context) and is intentionally NOT in targets to avoid double execution. tests/codex/test_execpolicy_matrix.py is included here so python-test and ci_test_selection/v1 both treat the pure-Python execpolicy contract coverage as part of the shared python lane, while the dedicated Codex CLI step keeps only the runtime guard test isolated. The Node-backed lane (pnpm test / e2e) and the hook-discovery collect-only guard remain separate steps; this plan only governs the unified python pytest suite.
notes.ignore_deselect_contract: ignore/deselect preserve the hook-discovery contract: test_secret_boundary_contract.py is ignored (tracked by #412) and the two non-hermetic generate_session_manifest wrapper tests are deselected.
notes.node_test_lane: test_session_manifest_debounce.mjs (AC4 virtual-clock tests) runs in the dedicated node-backed-hook-tests CI lane via `node --test` (Issue #1141 AC6). It is NOT in the pytest targets.
notes.edit_issue_tests_directory_coverage: Issue #1303: individual file targets test_guard_issue_body.py / test_issue_contract_hygiene_autofix.py were replaced by the directory target .claude/skills/edit-issue/tests/ so newly added tests in that directory (e.g. test_edit_issue_txn.py from PR #1301 / Issue #1287) are covered by the python-test lane and the ci_test_selection/v1 artifact without requiring a plan edit per new test file.
notes.runtime_verification_only_markers_ration: Issue #1562: pytest markers listed here are deselected from the default `python-test` pytest run (via pyproject.toml addopts, e.g. `github_live`) because they exercise real external services (e.g. `gh` write access to GitHub) that are structurally unavailable in the CI runner's authentication context. generate_ci_test_selection_artifact.py treats a changed test file whose tests are collected ONLY under one of these markers (verified by an actual `pytest --collect-only` re-run scoped to that file, both with and without the marker override) as covered by runtime verification rather than as a G1 `uncovered_changed_test_files` failure. This is deliberately distinct from `secondary_coverage.dedicated_lanes`, which declares coverage by a DIFFERENT CI job that actually executes the file; no such CI job exists for `github_live` tests, so registering them under dedicated_lanes would misrepresent a real CI job as covering tests it never runs.
